name: client
context: Send x402 stablecoin payments as an HTTP client using fetch, axios, or the Python/Go SDK
info: This skill enables any HTTP client or AI agent to automatically pay for resources protected by x402. The client wraps standard fetch or axios with x402 awareness: when a server returns 402, the client reads the payment requirements, signs an EIP-3009 or Permit2 authorization using the configured wallet, attaches it as a PAYMENT-SIGNATURE header, and retries. The entire flow is transparent — the caller just makes a normal HTTP request and gets back the resource. SDKs handle signing, header encoding, and retry logic. The client needs a funded wallet with USDC (or the required token) on the target chain.
triggers: pay for an api with x402
call a paid api with x402

sections.title: Fetch wrapper
sections.body: Install `@x402/fetch`. Create a viem wallet client from your private key (see [[ethereum/wallets#create-wallet]]). Call `wrapFetch(fetch, walletClient)` to get an x402-aware fetch. Use it exactly like `fetch()` — payment is handled transparently on 402 responses.

sections.title: Axios interceptor
sections.body: Install `@x402/axios`. Call `withX402(axios.create(), walletClient)` to get an x402-aware axios instance. Use standard axios methods — the interceptor handles 402 responses automatically.

sections.title: Python client
sections.body: Install `x402` via pip. Use `x402_fetch(url, private_key, chain)` for simple requests. The Python SDK handles signing, retry, and header encoding.

sections.title: Gasless payments
sections.body: When using EIP-3009 (USDC, EURC), the client only needs token balance — no ETH for gas (see [[ethereum/gas#l2-costs]] for fee context). The facilitator submits the transaction and pays gas (see [[ethereum/x402#facilitator]]). This makes x402 ideal for agents that hold only stablecoins.

related.note: Build the server side to accept payments from this client
Client needs a funded wallet for signing payments

scripts.name: x402 Client Request
scripts.description: Make an x402-aware HTTP request that auto-pays on 402

maxPrice.description: Maximum USD amount willing to pay per request

depends.reason: A funded wallet is required for signing payments

author: dojo-community
